perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) days of receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and verify in writing its destruction) all material embodying Confidential Information (in any form and including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information and all electronic media or records containing or derived from Confidential Information).
perturbation.changed_text: Within ten (10) days of receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return all original documents embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the 'destroy (and verify in writing its destruction) all material embodying Confidential Information (in any form and including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information and all electronic media or records containing or derived from Confidential Information)' the modified text contradicts and limits the scope of return, creating an inconsistency on all material, including destroying confidential information no longer valid. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 6, Ownership of Confidential Information which states: 'All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party.' because the changed text will now create a contradiction on the receiving party being able to disseminate this information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party will have no rights, by license or otherwise, to use or disseminate the Confidential Information except as expressly provided in this Agreement. No patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right is licensed, granted or otherwise conveyed by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to return or destroy confidential information. The changed text only requires returning original documents. This omission creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text, which states that 'All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party' and the Receiving Party cannot disseminate the information. The changed text allows the Receiving Party to retain copies and summaries of confidential information, thus conflicting with the Disclosing Party's exclusive ownership as stated in the Contradicted Text, thus falling under 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Party shall not be precluded from hiring any such employee, consultant, officer or director who (i) responds to any public advertisement placed by the Party or (ii) has been terminated by the other Party prior to commencement of employment discussions between the Party and such employee, consultant, officer or director.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Party shall not be precluded from hiring any such employee, consultant, officer or director who (i) responds to any public advertisement placed by the Party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting (ii) 'has been terminated by the other Party prior to commencement of employment discussions between the Party and such employee, consultant, officer or director.' from the non-solicit exceptions, there is an in-text contradiction because this creates an unclarity in the agreement because if one party terminates and employee, it is now vague if the other party can engage with the employee or not, thus creating a potential future violation of this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non Solicit. Each Party agrees that for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof, neither Party will, without the prior written consent of other, directly or indirectly solicit for employment or hire any employee, consultant, officer or director of the other Party with whom the Party has had contact or who became known to the Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Party shall not be precluded from hiring any such employee, consultant, officer or director who (i) responds to any public advertisement placed by the Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided an exception to the non-solicitation agreement, allowing a party to hire someone terminated by the other party. The changed text removes this exception. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text (which is identical to the original text) explicitly allows the hiring of terminated employees under certain conditions, while the changed text implicitly prohibits it by omitting the exception. This omission creates ambiguity about whether a party can hire an employee terminated by the other party, creating a potential breach and therefore an in-text contradiction as described in the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party further acknowledges and agrees that it has no liability to the other Party relating to or resulting from the use of the other Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party further acknowledges and agrees that it has no liability to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific clause 'relating to or resulting from the use of the other Party’s Confidential Information,' this creates a broader waiver of liability than originally intended. This contradicts the fundamental purpose of a confidentiality agreement, which is to protect the disclosing party from damages resulting from the misuse or unauthorized disclosure of their confidential information. If a party misuses confidential information and causes harm, this clause, as modified, suggests they may not be held liable, contradicting the inherent intent of Section 3, Protection of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Protection of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party agrees that it will use diligent efforts to protect the secrecy and confidentiality of and avoid disclosure of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, including implementing equivalent security measures and degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own proprietary or confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the waiver of liability to 'relating to or resulting from the use of the other Party’s Confidential Information.' The changed text broadens the waiver to include all liability, which now contradicts the 'Protection of Confidential Information' clause that requires the Receiving Party to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information. The modified text implies that a party misusing confidential information may not be held liable, directly conflicting with the intended protection outlined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees that disclosure or use of Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the owner thereof, for which monetary damages may be difficult to ascertain or be an inadequate remedy. Therefore, each Party agrees that the owner of Confidential Information may seek, in addition to its other rights and remedies, to seek injunctive relief for any violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees that disclosure or use of Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the owner thereof. Therefore, each Party agrees that the owner of Confidential Information may seek, in addition to its other rights and remedies, to seek injunctive relief for any violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'for which monetary damages may be difficult to ascertain or be an inadequate remedy', the argument for seeking injunctive relief is weakened. Injunctive relief is typically sought when monetary damages are insufficient. Removing this justification creates an in-text contradiction, because it removes the justification for injunctive relief in the first place. Section 16 covers 'Governing Law' with legal actions being brought. This changes suggests that the injunctive relief may no longer be the right course of action in comparison to governing law. This now creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia and any legal action hereunder may be brought in an appropriate federal or state court located in Fairfax County, Virginia. The prevailing Party in any such action shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs incurred in any such action.
perturbation.justification: The original text included the phrase 'for which monetary damages may be difficult to ascertain or be an inadequate remedy', which justified seeking injunctive relief. By omitting this phrase, the changed text weakens the justification for injunctive relief. This creates a contradiction with the 'Governing Law' section, as it makes the appropriateness of seeking injunctive relief questionable compared to pursuing legal action in the specified courts. The Perturbation Explanation section states that this omission suggests that injunctive relief may no longer be the right course of action in comparison to governing law, creating an in-text contradiction because the governing law clause outlines how legal action will be handled.

perturbation.original_text: Further, all terms and conditions of this Agreement shall be deemed enforceable to the fullest extent permissible under applicable law, and when necessary, the court is requested to reform any and all terms or conditions to give them such effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Further, all terms and conditions of this Agreement shall be deemed enforceable under applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'and when necessary, the court is requested to reform any and all terms or conditions to give them such effect', this removes the court's explicit instruction or permission to reform the contract if a term is deemed unenforceable. This creates an in-text contradiction, because Section 17, 'Severability', allows that 'If a provision of this Agreement is held invalid under any applicable law, such invalidity shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement that can be given effect without the invalid provision'. With the reform term, the severability, creates an unclarity in the agreement and an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Severability. If a provision of this Agreement is held invalid under any applicable law, such invalidity shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement that can be given effect without the invalid provision. Further, all terms and conditions of this Agreement shall be deemed enforceable under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the court to reform the agreement if a term was unenforceable, which provided an avenue to maintain the overall agreement's validity, despite individual term issues. The changed text removes this option. Section 17 (the contradicted text) allows severability, meaning an invalid provision doesn't affect others if the agreement can function without it. The original text's reformation clause worked in conjunction with severability. By removing the court's power to reform, the agreement creates uncertainty as to whether the severability clause can be fully utilized, because it is unclear if the court is able to reform the agreement. The change thus generates a strong in-text contradiction because it impacts how severability can be applied, creating uncertainty and a conflict within the document.
